metadata.name: recurly__account_daily_overview
metadata.comment: Each record is a day in an account and its accumulated balance totals based on all line item transactions up to that day.

account_created_at.name: account_created_at
account_created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the account entry was created.

account_city.name: account_city
account_city.comment: City in the world the account is associated with.

account_company.name: account_company
account_company.comment: The company related with the account.

account_country.name: account_country
account_country.comment: Country in the world the account is associated with.

account_code.name: account_code
account_code.comment: The unique identifier of the account. This cannot be changed once the account is created.  Provided during account creation.

account_email.name: account_email
account_email.comment: Email address associated with the account.

account_first_name.name: account_first_name
account_first_name.comment: First name associated with the account.

account_last_name.name: account_last_name
account_last_name.comment: Last name associated with the account.

account_is_tax_exempt.name: account_is_tax_exempt
account_is_tax_exempt.comment: The tax status of the account. true exempts tax on the account, false applies tax on the account.

account_postal_code.name: account_postal_code
account_postal_code.comment: Postal code in the world the account is associated with.

account_region.name: account_region
account_region.comment: Region in the world the account is associated with (usually would be state if it was a U.S. account.)

account_username.name: account_username
account_username.comment: A secondary value for the account.

account_daily_id.name: account_daily_id
account_daily_id.comment: Unique account key, based on source_relation, account id, and date of balance data.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: Date compiled transactions by day for this account balance took place.

date_week.name: date_week
date_week.comment: Date compiled transactions by week for this account balance took place.

date_month.name: date_month
date_month.comment: Date compiled transactions by month for this account balance took place.

date_year.name: date_year
date_year.comment: Date compiled transactions by year for this account balance took place.

date_index.name: date_index
date_index.comment: The nth date in the transaction model.

daily_transaction_count.name: daily_transaction_count
daily_transaction_count.comment: Number of transactions that took place this day for this account.

daily_net_change.name: daily_net_change
daily_net_change.comment: Total sum of all transactions that took place this day for this account and the balance change that ensued.

daily_invoice_count.name: daily_invoice_count
daily_invoice_count.comment: Number of invoices that day for this account.

daily_charges.name: daily_charges
daily_charges.comment: Total sum of all charges that took place this day for this account.

daily_credits.name: daily_credits
daily_credits.comment: Total sum of all credits that took place this day for this account.

daily_discounts.name: daily_discounts
daily_discounts.comment: Total sum of all discounts that took place this day for this account.

daily_taxes.name: daily_taxes
daily_taxes.comment: Total sum of all taxes that took place this day for this account.

daily_charge_count.name: daily_charge_count
daily_charge_count.comment: Number of charges that day for this account.

daily_credit_count.name: daily_credit_count
daily_credit_count.comment: Number of credits that day for this account.

rolling_account_balance.name: rolling_account_balance
rolling_account_balance.comment: Accumulated account balance based on previous daily transactions on this account.

rolling_invoices.name: rolling_invoices
rolling_invoices.comment: Number of invoices on this account since it was created to the current date.

rolling_transactions.name: rolling_transactions
rolling_transactions.comment: Number of transactions on this account since it was created to the current date.

rolling_charge_balance.name: rolling_charge_balance
rolling_charge_balance.comment: Accumulated charge balance based on previous daily charges on this account.

rolling_credit_balance.name: rolling_credit_balance
rolling_credit_balance.comment: Accumulated credit balance based on previous daily credits on this account.

rolling_discount_balance.name: rolling_discount_balance
rolling_discount_balance.comment: Accumulated discount balance based on previous daily discounts on this account.

rolling_tax_balance.name: rolling_tax_balance
rolling_tax_balance.comment: Accumulated tax balance based on previous daily taxes on this account.

rolling_charges.name: rolling_charges
rolling_charges.comment: Number of charges on this account since it was created to the current date.

rolling_credits.name: rolling_credits
rolling_credits.comment: Number of credits on this account since it was created to the current date.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__account_overview
metadata.comment: Each record represents an account, enriched with metrics about their associated transactions.

account_created_at.name: account_created_at
account_created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the account entry was created.

account_city.name: account_city
account_city.comment: City in the world the account is associated with.

account_code.name: account_code
account_code.comment: The unique identifier of the account. This cannot be changed once the account is created.  Provided during account creation.

account_company.name: account_company
account_company.comment: The company related with the account.

account_country.name: account_country
account_country.comment: Country in the world the account is associated with.

account_email.name: account_email
account_email.comment: Email address associated with the account.

account_first_name.name: account_first_name
account_first_name.comment: First name associated with the account.

account_is_tax_exempt.name: account_is_tax_exempt
account_is_tax_exempt.comment: The tax status of the account. true exempts tax on the account, false applies tax on the account.

account_last_name.name: account_last_name
account_last_name.comment: Last name associated with the account.

account_postal_code.name: account_postal_code
account_postal_code.comment: Postal code in the world the account is associated with.

account_region.name: account_region
account_region.comment: Region in the world the account is associated with (usually would be state if it was a U.S. account.)

account_username.name: account_username
account_username.comment: A secondary value for the account.

total_transactions.name: total_transactions
total_transactions.comment: Total amount of unique transactions issued to this account.

total_invoices.name: total_invoices
total_invoices.comment: Total amount of unique invoices issued to this account.

total_charges.name: total_charges
total_charges.comment: Total amount of charges issued to this account.

total_credits.name: total_credits
total_credits.comment: Total amount of credits issued to this account.

total_balance.name: total_balance
total_balance.comment: Gross amount of all transactions made with this account.

total_discounts.name: total_discounts
total_discounts.comment: Total amount of discounts issued to this account.

total_taxes.name: total_taxes
total_taxes.comment: Total amount of taxes issued to this account.

total_charge_count.name: total_charge_count
total_charge_count.comment: Total number of charges issued to this account.

total_credit_count.name: total_credit_count
total_credit_count.comment: Total number of credits issued to this account.

transactions_this_month.name: transactions_this_month
transactions_this_month.comment: All transactions on this account from this current calendar month.

invoices_this_month.name: invoices_this_month
invoices_this_month.comment: All invoices on this account from this current calendar month.

charges_this_month.name: charges_this_month
charges_this_month.comment: All charges on this account from this current calendar month.

credits_this_month.name: credits_this_month
credits_this_month.comment: All credits on this account from this current calendar month.

balance_this_month.name: balance_this_month
balance_this_month.comment: Gross amount of all transactions made on this account from this current calendar month.

discounts_this_month.name: discounts_this_month
discounts_this_month.comment: Discounts issued on the account from this current calendar month.

taxes_this_month.name: taxes_this_month
taxes_this_month.comment: Taxes issued on the account from this current calendar month.

first_charge_date.name: first_charge_date
first_charge_date.comment: Date the first charge on the account was issued.

most_recent_charge_date.name: most_recent_charge_date
most_recent_charge_date.comment: Date the most recent charge on the account was issued.

first_invoice_date.name: first_invoice_date
first_invoice_date.comment: Date the first invoice on the account was issued.

most_recent_invoice_date.name: most_recent_invoice_date
most_recent_invoice_date.comment: Date the most recent invoice on the account was issued.

next_invoice_due_at.name: next_invoice_due_at
next_invoice_due_at.comment: Date the most recent invoice on the account is due at.

first_transaction_date.name: first_transaction_date
first_transaction_date.comment: Date first transaction occurred on the account.

most_recent_transaction_date.name: most_recent_transaction_date
most_recent_transaction_date.comment: Date most recent transaction occurred on the account.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__balance_transactions
metadata.comment: Each record represents a specific line item charge, credit, or other balance change that accumulates into the final invoices.

balance_transaction_id.name: balance_transaction_id
balance_transaction_id.comment: Unique identifier for the balance transaction line item

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: When the line item was created.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When the line item was last updated.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: Customer in Recurly responsible for the line item.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: Invoice in Recurly these line items are associated with.

invoice_number.name: invoice_number
invoice_number.comment: If VAT taxation and the Country Invoice Sequencing feature are enabled,  invoices will have country-specific invoice numbers for invoices billed to EU countries (e.g. FR1001).  Non-EU invoices will continue to use the site-level invoice number sequence.

type.name: type
type.comment: Charges are positive line items that debit the account.  Credits are negative line items that credit the account. Enum: "charge", "credit"

state.name: state
state.comment: Pending line items are charges or credits on an account that have not been applied to an invoice yet.  Invoiced line items will always have an invoice_id value. Enum: "invoiced", "pending"

origin.name: origin
origin.comment: A credit created from an original charge will have the value of the charge's origin

product_code.name: product_code
product_code.comment: For plan-related line items this will be the plan's code, for add-on related line items it will be the add-on's code.  For item-related line items it will be the item's external_sku.

discount.name: discount
discount.comment: The discount applied to the line item.

tax.name: tax
tax.comment: The tax amount for the line item.

description.name: description
description.comment: Description that appears on the invoice. For subscription related items this will be filled in automatically.

plan_code.name: plan_code
plan_code.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for a plan or add-on, this is the plan's code.

add_on_code.name: add_on_code
add_on_code.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for an add-on, this is its code.

has_refund.name: has_refund
has_refund.comment: True if the line item is refund, false if it is not.

refunded_quantity.name: refunded_quantity
refunded_quantity.comment: For refund charges, the quantity being refunded. For non-refund charges, the total quantity refunded (possibly over multiple refunds).

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Total after discounts and taxes (quantity * unit_amount) - (discount + tax).

credit_applied.name: credit_applied
credit_applied.comment: The amount of credit from this line item that was applied to the invoice.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: This number will be multiplied by the unit amount to compute the subtotal before any discounts or taxes.

unit_amount.name: unit_amount
unit_amount.comment: Positive amount for a charge, negative amount for a credit.

subtotal.name: subtotal
subtotal.comment: Total before discounts and taxes (quantity * unit_amount).

started_at.name: started_at
started_at.comment: If an end date is present, this is value indicates the beginning of a billing time range.  If no end date is present it indicates billing for a specific date.

ended_at.name: ended_at
ended_at.comment: If this date is provided, it indicates the end of a time range

original_line_item_invoice_id.name: original_line_item_invoice_id
original_line_item_invoice_id.comment: The invoice where the credit originated.  Will only have a value if the line item is a credit created from a previous credit,  or if the credit was created from a charge refund.

previous_line_item_id.name: previous_line_item_id
previous_line_item_id.comment: Will only have a value if the line item is a credit created from a previous credit,  or if the credit was created from a charge refund.

invoice_origin.name: invoice_origin
invoice_origin.comment: The event that created the invoice. Enum: "credit", "gift_card", "immediate_change", "line_item_refund", "open_amount_refund",  "purchase", "renewal", "termination", "write_off", "prepayment".

invoice_due_at.name: invoice_due_at
invoice_due_at.comment: Date invoice is due. This is the date the net terms are reached.

invoice_closed_at.name: invoice_closed_at
invoice_closed_at.comment: Date invoice was marked paid or failed.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.comment: The id of the transaction associated with the invoice.

transaction_type.name: transaction_type
transaction_type.comment:
Transaction types:
  authorization: verifies billing information and places a hold on money in the customer's account.
  capture: captures funds held by an authorization and completes a purchase.
  purchase: combines the authorization and capture in one transaction.
  refund: returns all or a portion of the money collected in a previous transaction to the customer.
  verify: a $0 or $1 transaction used to verify billing information which is immediately voided.
Enum: "authorization", "capture", "purchase", "refund", "verify"

transaction_origin.name: transaction_origin
transaction_origin.comment: Describes how the transaction was triggered. Enum: "api", "chargeback", "force_collect", "hpp", "merchant", "recurly_admin", "recurlyjs", "recurring", "refunded_externally", "transparent"

transaction_status.name: transaction_status
transaction_status.comment: The current transaction status. Note that the status may change, e.g. a pending transaction may become declined or success may later become void. Enum: "chargeback", "declined", "error", "pending", "processing", "scheduled", "success", "void"

transaction_status_message.name: transaction_status_message
transaction_status_message.comment: For declined (success=false) transactions, the message displayed to the merchant.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__churn_analysis
metadata.comment: Each record represents a subscription and their churn status and details.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account this subscription belongs to.

canceled_at.name: canceled_at
canceled_at.comment: When the subscription was canceled. Can take future dates.

expires_at.name: expires_at
expires_at.comment: When the subscription has expired. Can take future dates.

has_auto_renew.name: has_auto_renew
has_auto_renew.comment: Whether the subscription renews at the end of its term. Default: true

plan_name.name: plan_name
plan_name.comment: This name describes your plan and will appear on the Hosted Payment Page and the subscriber's invoice.

plan_state.name: plan_state
plan_state.comment: The current state of the plan. Enum: "active", "inactive"

subscription_end_date.name: subscription_end_date
subscription_end_date.comment: When the subscription actually ends, factors in cancellations earlier than the expected end date.

subscription_interval_days.name: subscription_interval_days
subscription_interval_days.comment: Subscription interval by number of days, accounting for cancelled/expired periods.

subscription_period.name: subscription_period
subscription_period.comment: The nth subscription defined by the time period set. Subscriptions start with month 0.

subscription_state.name: subscription_state
subscription_state.comment: The current state of the subscription. Enum: "active", "canceled", "expired", "failed", "future", "paused"

churn_reason.name: churn_reason
churn_reason.comment: Reason for churn. null if subscription has no expiration date. Enum: "account closed", "canceled", "gift ended", "non-payment", "non-renewing", "trial ended"

churn_reason_type.name: churn_reason_type
churn_reason_type.comment: Type of churn, null if subscription has no expiration date. Enum: "involuntary", "voluntary"

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__line_item_enhanced
metadata.comment: This model constructs a comprehensive, denormalized analytical table that enables reporting on key revenue, subscription, customer, and product metrics from your billing platform. It's designed to align with the schema of the `*__line_item_enhanced` model found in Recurly, Recharge, Stripe, Shopify, and Zuora, offering standardized reporting across various billing platforms. To see the kinds of insights this model can generate, explore example visualizations in the [Fivetran Billing Model Streamlit App](https://fivetran-billing-model.streamlit.app/). Visit the app for more details.

line_item_index.name: line_item_index
line_item_index.comment: Unique index of each line item id for each invoice.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.comment: ID of the plan associated with the line item.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: The number of units of this line item.

discount_amount.name: discount_amount
discount_amount.comment: The discount amount associated with this invoice.

tax_amount.name: tax_amount
tax_amount.comment: The tax amount associated with this invoice.

payment_method_id.name: payment_method_id
payment_method_id.comment: In Recurly there are no payment method IDs as these are abstracted from the transactions object. Therefore, this will always be null.

fee_amount.name: fee_amount
fee_amount.comment: In Recurly there is currently no concept of fees. Therefore, this will be null for all records.

refund_amount.name: refund_amount
refund_amount.comment: Refund amount associated with this invoice.

subscription_plan.name: subscription_plan
subscription_plan.comment: The name of the plan which the subscription is associated.

customer_created_at.name: customer_created_at
customer_created_at.comment: Date which the customer was created in Recurly.

customer_level.name: customer_level
customer_level.comment: Whether 'account' or 'customer'. For Recurly, the level is 'account'.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__monthly_recurring_revenue
metadata.comment: Each record represents an account and MRR generated on a monthly basis.

account_monthly_id.name: account_monthly_id
account_monthly_id.comment: Unique identifier associated by source_relation, account id, and month.

account_month.name: account_month
account_month.comment: Revenue details of the account by month.

account_month_number.name: account_month_number
account_month_number.comment: The nth month the account has been active.

current_month_mrr.name: current_month_mrr
current_month_mrr.comment: The current month's recurring revenue earned by account.

previous_month_mrr.name: previous_month_mrr
previous_month_mrr.comment: The previous month's recurring revenue earned by account.

account_code.name: account_code
account_code.comment: The unique identifier of the account. This cannot be changed once the account is created. Provided during account creation.

account_email.name: account_email
account_email.comment: The email address used for communicating with this customer.  The customer will also use this email address to log into your hosted account management pages.  This value does not need to be unique.

account_first_name.name: account_first_name
account_first_name.comment: The first name of the customer related to the account.

account_last_name.name: account_last_name
account_last_name.comment: The last name of the customer related to the account.

account_username.name: account_username
account_username.comment: A secondary value for the account.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: recurly__subscription_overview
metadata.comment: Each record represents a subscription, enriched with metrics about time, revenue, state, and period.

subscription_key.name: subscription_key
subscription_key.comment: Unique identifier for the subscription daily overview table, based on source_relation, subscription_id, and updated_at.

add_ons_total.name: add_ons_total
add_ons_total.comment: Total price of add-ons. Greater or equal to 0.

canceled_at.name: canceled_at
canceled_at.comment: When the subscription was canceled. Can take future dates.

expires_at.name: expires_at
expires_at.comment: When the subscription has expired. Can take future dates.

has_auto_renew.name: has_auto_renew
has_auto_renew.comment: Whether the subscription renews at the end of its term. Default: true

subscription_period.name: subscription_period
subscription_period.comment: The nth subscription defined by the time period set. Subscriptions start with month 0.

subscription_state.name: subscription_state
subscription_state.comment: The current state of the subscription. Enum: "active", "canceled", "expired", "failed", "future", "paused"

subscription_end_date.name: subscription_end_date
subscription_end_date.comment: When the subscription actually ends, factors in cancellations earlier than the expected end date.

subscription_interval_days.name: subscription_interval_days
subscription_interval_days.comment: Subscription interval by number of days, accounting for cancelled/expired periods.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account this subscription belongs to.

account_created_at.name: account_created_at
account_created_at.comment: The date this account was created.

account_email.name: account_email
account_email.comment: Email address associated with the account for this subscription.

account_first_name.name: account_first_name
account_first_name.comment: First name associated with the account for this subscription.

account_last_name.name: account_last_name
account_last_name.comment: Last name associated with the account for this subscription.

plan_code.name: plan_code
plan_code.comment: Unique code to identify the plan. This is used in Hosted Payment Page URLs and in the invoice exports.

plan_interval_days.name: plan_interval_days
plan_interval_days.comment: The number of days on the expected Recurly plan.

plan_is_tax_exempt.name: plan_is_tax_exempt
plan_is_tax_exempt.comment: true exempts tax on the plan, false applies tax on the plan.

plan_name.name: plan_name
plan_name.comment: This name describes your plan and will appear on the Hosted Payment Page and the subscriber's invoice.

plan_state.name: plan_state
plan_state.comment: The current state of the plan. Enum: "active", "inactive"

plan_total_billing_cycles.name: plan_total_billing_cycles
plan_total_billing_cycles.comment: Automatically terminate subscriptions after a defined number of billing cycles.  Number of billing cycles before the plan automatically stops renewing, defaults to null for continuous, automatic renewal.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__account_balance_history
metadata.comment: The customer account balance is made up of any open or past due invoices on the account alongside any charge or credit adjustments that are left uninvoiced and open on the account respectively.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account the balance update is associated with.

account_updated_at.name: account_updated_at
account_updated_at.comment: When the account balance was last changed.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: The combined amount of charge or credit adjustments that are left uninvoiced and open on the account.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code (USD for US Dollar)

past_due.name: past_due
past_due.comment: Is the existing amount of this balance due?

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__account_history
metadata.comment: The account object stores the entire Recurly history of your customer and acts as the entry point for working with a customer's billing information, subscription data, transactions, invoices and more. Once an account is created, all values (with the exception of the account code) may be updated.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: Uniquely identifies your customers in Recurly. No two customers can share the same account id. Part of the PK

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When the account was last changed. Part of the PK

account_city.name: account_city
account_city.comment: City in the world the account is associated with.

account_country.name: account_country
account_country.comment: Country in the world the account is associated with.

account_postal_code.name: account_postal_code
account_postal_code.comment: Postal code in the world the account is associated with.

account_region.name: account_region
account_region.comment: Region in the world the account is associated with (usually would be state if it was a U.S. account.)

bill_to.name: bill_to
bill_to.comment: An enumerable describing the billing behavior of the account,  specifically whether the account is self-paying or will rely on the parent account to pay.

cc_emails.name: cc_emails
cc_emails.comment: Additional email address that should receive account correspondence.  These should be separated only by commas.  These CC emails will receive all emails that the email field also receives.

code.name: code
code.comment: The unique identifier of the account. This cannot be changed once the account is created.  Provided during account creation.

company.name: company
company.comment: The company related with the account.

deleted_at.name: deleted_at
deleted_at.comment: If present, when the account was last marked inactive.

email.name: email
email.comment: The email address used for communicating with this customer.  The customer will also use this email address to log into your hosted account management pages.  This value does not need to be unique.

first_name.name: first_name
first_name.comment: The first name of the customer related to the account.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this account?

is_tax_exempt.name: is_tax_exempt
is_tax_exempt.comment: The tax status of the account. true exempts tax on the account, false applies tax on the account.

last_name.name: last_name
last_name.comment: The last name of the customer related to the account.

state.name: state
state.comment: Accounts can be either active or inactive.

username.name: username
username.comment: A secondary value for the account.

vat_number.name: vat_number
vat_number.comment: The VAT number of the account (to avoid having the VAT applied). This is only used for manually collected invoices.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__account_note_history
metadata.comment: A history of notes your team can leave on an account to add context. Notes are internal and not exposed to customers.

account_note_id.name: account_note_id
account_note_id.comment: Uniquely identifies the account note created.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: Account associated with the note created.

account_updated_at.name: account_updated_at
account_updated_at.comment: Last time the account note was updated.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Date/time When the note was created.

user_email.name: user_email
user_email.comment: Email associated with who created the note.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.comment: Id associated with who created the note.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__billing_info_history
metadata.comment: Stored payment information for an account. Filled out by the customer upon purchase or when they update information.

billing_id.name: billing_id
billing_id.comment: Unique identifier for the billing info object.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Date customer's billing information was last updated on the account.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: Account identifier for the billing info.

billing_city.name: billing_city
billing_city.comment: Billing city of the card on file for the account.

billing_country.name: billing_country
billing_country.comment: Billing country of the card on file for the account.

billing_postal_code.name: billing_postal_code
billing_postal_code.comment: Billing postal code of the card on file for the account.

billing_region.name: billing_region
billing_region.comment: Billing region of the card on file for the account, like state in the US or province in Canada.

billing_street_1.name: billing_street_1
billing_street_1.comment: First address line of the card on file for the account.

billing_street_2.name: billing_street_2
billing_street_2.comment: Second address line of the card on file for the account.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Date customer's billing information was added to the account.

first_name.name: first_name
first_name.comment: First name of the cardholder for the account.

is_valid.name: is_valid
is_valid.comment: Is the card valid? Boolean object.

last_name.name: last_name
last_name.comment: Last name of the cardholder for the account.

payment_method_card_type.name: payment_method_card_type
payment_method_card_type.comment: Card type of payment method, like Visa or Mastercard.

payment_method_object.name: payment_method_object
payment_method_object.comment: Object type of payment method, like credit card or debit card.

updated_by_country.name: updated_by_country
updated_by_country.comment: Country from which latest billing info update came from.

updated_by_ip.name: updated_by_ip
updated_by_ip.comment: IP address from which latest billing info update came from.

vat_number.name: vat_number
vat_number.comment: (for EU companies), VAT number provider by customer.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__coupon_discount
metadata.comment: Coupon details and the discounts set for customer purchases.

coupon_id.name: coupon_id
coupon_id.comment: Coupon details and the discounts set for customer purchases.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Fixed amount discount being applied, if applicable. Percentage would be null if amount exists.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code (USD for US Dollar).

fivetran_id.name: fivetran_id
fivetran_id.comment: Combined unique surrogate key for the model.

percentage.name: percentage
percentage.comment: Percentage discount being applied, if applicable. Amount would be null if percentage exists.

trial_length.name: trial_length
trial_length.comment: Period of time that coupon will discount eligible purchases by customer.

trial_unit.name: trial_unit
trial_unit.comment: Unit of time associated with trial time ('day', 'week', 'month')

type.name: type
type.comment: Delineates between which type of discount is being applied ('fixed' for amount discount, 'percent' for percentage discount)

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__coupon_redemption_history
metadata.comment: History of coupons redeemed by accounts.

coupon_redemption_id.name: coupon_redemption_id
coupon_redemption_id.comment: Unique identifier for the coupon redemption created.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: Account associated with the coupon being redeemed.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code (USD for US Dollar).

removed_at.name: removed_at
removed_at.comment: If a coupon is removed from a customer, time of removal.

state.name: state
state.comment: Current state of coupon redemption (usually active or inactive)

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__credit_payment_history
metadata.comment: A history of outstanding credit balance to pay an invoice, but does include the record of write-offs, credit balance removals, and credit payments later refunded as cash transaction.

credit_payment_id.name: credit_payment_id
credit_payment_id.comment: Unique identifier for the credit payment.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The id of the account the credit payment is associated with.

action.name: action
action.comment: The action that resulted in the credit payment being created. Enum: "payment", "gift_card", "write_off", "reduction", "refund"

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: The amount of the credit payment, which will always be positive.

applied_to_invoice_id.name: applied_to_invoice_id
applied_to_invoice_id.comment: The invoice the credit payment was applied to.  If action is payment, gift_card, or write_off, this is a charge invoice.  If action is reduction or refund, this is a credit invoice.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The date and time the credit payment was created.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code (USD for US Dollar).

refund_transaction_id.name: refund_transaction_id
refund_transaction_id.comment: The unique id of the new refund transaction the refund action credit payment is transferring value to.  Will only populate if the action on the row is "refund".

original_credit_payment_id.name: original_credit_payment_id
original_credit_payment_id.comment: The unique id of the credit payment the refund action credit payment is refunding.  Will only populate if the action on the row is "refund".

original_invoice_id.name: original_invoice_id
original_invoice_id.comment: The invoice of the credit invoice the credit payment came from.

voided_at.name: voided_at
voided_at.comment: Time when a credit payment is voided.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__invoice_coupon_redemption_history
metadata.comment: History of coupons redeemed and applied to specific invoices.

coupon_redemption_id.name: coupon_redemption_id
coupon_redemption_id.comment: Unique identifier for the credit payment.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: Invoice the coupon redemption is associated with.

invoice_updated_at.name: invoice_updated_at
invoice_updated_at.comment: Last time the invoice was updated.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__invoice_history
metadata.comment: An invoice relates charges, credits, and payments together. When a subscription is created or renewed or a charge is created on the account, Recurly will sum the charges, discount or tax as appropriate, and send the invoice out for collection. The table holds the lineage of the invoice.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When the invoice was last updated.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account this invoice belongs to.

balance.name: balance
balance.comment: The outstanding balance remaining on this invoice.

closed_at.name: closed_at
closed_at.comment: Date invoice was marked paid or failed.

collection_method.name: collection_method
collection_method.comment: An automatic invoice means a corresponding transaction is run using the account's billing information  at the same time the invoice is created. Manual invoices are created without a corresponding transaction.  The merchant must enter a manual payment transaction or have the customer pay the invoice with an automatic method,  like credit card, PayPal, Amazon, or ACH bank payment. Default: "automatic" Enum: "automatic", "manual"

discount.name: discount
discount.comment: Total discounts applied to this invoice.

due_at.name: due_at
due_at.comment: Date invoice is due. This is the date the net terms are reached.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this invoice?

net_terms.name: net_terms
net_terms.comment: Integer representing the number of days after an invoice's creation that the invoice will become past due.  If an invoice's net terms are set to '0', it is due 'On Receipt' and will become past due 24 hours after it’s created.  If an invoice is due net 30, it will become past due at 31 days exactly. Default: 0

number.name: number
number.comment: If VAT taxation and the Country Invoice Sequencing feature are enabled,  invoices will have country-specific invoice numbers for invoices billed to EU countries (e.g. FR1001).  Non-EU invoices will continue to use the site-level invoice number sequence.

origin.name: origin
origin.comment: The event that created the invoice. Enum: "credit", "gift_card", "immediate_change", "line_item_refund", "open_amount_refund",  "purchase", "renewal", "termination", "write_off", "prepayment".

paid.name: paid
paid.comment: The total amount of successful payments transaction on this invoice.

po_number.name: po_number
po_number.comment: For manual invoicing, this identifies the PO number associated with the subscription.

previous_invoice_id.name: previous_invoice_id
previous_invoice_id.comment: On refund invoices, this value will exist and show the invoice ID of the purchase invoice the refund was created from.

refundable_amount.name: refundable_amount
refundable_amount.comment: The refundable amount on a charge invoice. It will be null for all other invoices.

subtotal.name: subtotal
subtotal.comment: The summation of charges, discounts, and credits, before tax.

tax.name: tax
tax.comment: The total tax on this invoice.

tax_region.name: tax_region
tax_region.comment: Provides the tax region applied on an invoice. For U.S. Sales Tax,  this will be the 2 letter state code.  For EU VAT this will be the 2 letter country code.  For all country level tax types, this will display the regional tax, like VAT, GST, or PST.

tax_type.name: tax_type
tax_type.comment: Provides the tax type as "vat" for EU VAT, "usst" for U.S. Sales Tax,  or the 2 letter country code for country level tax types like Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and all non-EU European countries.

total.name: total
total.comment: The final total on this invoice. The summation of invoice charges, discounts, credits, and tax. Alternative the summation of subtotal and tax.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__invoice_subscription_history
metadata.comment: The connection between invoices and subscription. FK on invoice are both invoice_id and invoice_updated_at, and on subscription is subscription_id. The table holds the lineage of the invoice - subscription connection.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The id of the invoice associated with the subscription.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: The id of the subscription associated with the invoice.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__line_item_history
metadata.comment: Line items are the charges and credits on your customer's invoices. The table holds the lineage of the line item.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When the line item was last updated.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account this line item belongs to.

add_on_code.name: add_on_code
add_on_code.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for an add-on, this is its code.

add_on_id.name: add_on_id
add_on_id.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for an add-on this is its ID.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Total after discounts and taxes (quantity * unit_amount) - (discount + tax).

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: When the line item was created.

credit_applied.name: credit_applied
credit_applied.comment: The amount of credit from this line item that was applied to the invoice.

description.name: description
description.comment: Description that appears on the invoice. For subscription related items this will be filled in automatically.

discount.name: discount
discount.comment: The discount applied to the line item.

ended_at.name: ended_at
ended_at.comment: If this date is provided, it indicates the end of a time range

has_refund.name: has_refund
has_refund.comment: Is the line item refunded? True if so, false if not.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The invoice this line item belongs to, once the line item has been invoiced this will be the invoice's ID.

invoice_number.name: invoice_number
invoice_number.comment: The invoice this line item belongs to, once the line item has been invoiced this will be the invoice's number.

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this line item?

is_taxable.name: is_taxable
is_taxable.comment: Is the line item taxable? True if so, false if not.

original_line_item_invoice_id.name: original_line_item_invoice_id
original_line_item_invoice_id.comment: The invoice where the credit originated.  Will only have a value if the line item is a credit created from a previous credit,  or if the credit was created from a charge refund.

origin.name: origin
origin.comment: A credit created from an original charge will have the value of the charge's origin.

plan_code.name: plan_code
plan_code.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for a plan or add-on, this is the plan's code.

plan_id.name: plan_id
plan_id.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for a plan or add-on, this is the plan's ID.

previous_line_item_id.name: previous_line_item_id
previous_line_item_id.comment: Will only have a value if the line item is a credit created from a previous credit,  or if the credit was created from a charge refund.

product_code.name: product_code
product_code.comment: For plan-related line items this will be the plan's code, for add-on related line items it will be the add-on's code.  For item-related line items it will be the item's external_sku.

proration_rate.name: proration_rate
proration_rate.comment: When a line item has been prorated, this is the rate of the proration.  Proration rates were made available for line items created after March 30, 2017.  For line items created prior to that date, the proration rate will be null, even if the line item was prorated.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: This number will be multiplied by the unit amount to compute the subtotal before any discounts or taxes.

refunded_quantity.name: refunded_quantity
refunded_quantity.comment: For refund charges, the quantity being refunded. For non-refund charges, the total quantity refunded (possibly over multiple refunds).

started_at.name: started_at
started_at.comment: If an end date is present, this is value indicates the beginning of a billing time range.  If no end date is present it indicates billing for a specific date.

state.name: state
state.comment: Pending line items are charges or credits on an account that have not been applied to an invoice yet.  Invoiced line items will always have an invoice_id value. Enum: "invoiced", "pending"

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: If the line item is a charge or credit for a subscription, this is its ID.

subtotal.name: subtotal
subtotal.comment: Total before discounts and taxes (quantity * unit_amount).

tax.name: tax
tax.comment: The tax amount for the line item.

tax_code.name: tax_code
tax_code.comment: Used by Avalara, Vertex, and Recurly’s EU VAT tax feature. The tax code values are specific to each tax system.  If you are using Recurly’s EU VAT feature you can use unknown, physical, or digital.:

tax_exempt.name: tax_exempt
tax_exempt.comment: true exempts tax on charges, false applies tax on charges. If not defined, then defaults to the Plan and Site settings.

tax_region.name: tax_region
tax_region.comment: Provides the tax region applied on an invoice.  For U.S. Sales Tax, this will be the 2 letter state code.  For EU VAT this will be the 2 letter country code.  For all country level tax types, this will display the regional tax, like VAT, GST, or PST.

tax_type.name: tax_type
tax_type.comment: Provides the tax type as "vat" for EU VAT, "usst" for U.S. Sales Tax, or the 2 letter country code for country level tax types  like Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and all non-EU European countries.

type.name: type
type.comment: Charges are positive line items that debit the account.  Credits are negative line items that credit the account. Enum: "charge", "credit"

unit_amount.name: unit_amount
unit_amount.comment: Positive amount for a charge, negative amount for a credit.

uuid.name: uuid
uuid.comment: The UUID is useful for matching data with the CSV exports and building URLs into Recurly's UI. Used in HTB database.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__plan_currency_history
metadata.comment: Plan prices

setup_fees.name: setup_fees
setup_fees.comment: Fee automatically charged at the beginning of a subscription billing cycle.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__plan_history
metadata.comment: Plans define the base price, currency, and billing cycle for recurring purchases of products. The table holds the lineage of the plan.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: When the plan was last updated.

accounting_code.name: accounting_code
accounting_code.comment: Accounting code for invoice line items for the plan. If no value is provided, it defaults to plan's code.

code.name: code
code.comment: Unique code to identify the plan. This is used in Hosted Payment Page URLs and in the invoice exports.

has_auto_renew.name: has_auto_renew
has_auto_renew.comment: Subscriptions will automatically inherit this value once they are active.  If auto_renew is true, then a subscription will automatically renew its term at renewal.  If auto_renew is false, then a subscription will expire at the end of its term.  auto_renew can be overridden on the subscription record itself. Default: true

interval_length.name: interval_length
interval_length.comment: Length of the plan's billing interval in interval_unit. Default: 1

interval_unit.name: interval_unit
interval_unit.comment: Unit for the plan's billing interval. Default: "months" Enum: "days", "months"

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this plan?

is_tax_exempt.name: is_tax_exempt
is_tax_exempt.comment: true exempts tax on the plan, false applies tax on the plan.

name.name: name
name.comment: This name describes your plan and will appear on the Hosted Payment Page and the subscriber's invoice.

setup_fee_accounting_code.name: setup_fee_accounting_code
setup_fee_accounting_code.comment: Accounting code for invoice line items for the plan's setup fee. If no value is provided, it defaults to plan's accounting code.

state.name: state
state.comment: The current state of the plan. Enum: "active", "inactive"

tax_code.name: tax_code
tax_code.comment: Used by Avalara, Vertex, and Recurly’s EU VAT tax feature. The tax code values are specific to each tax system.  If you are using Recurly’s EU VAT feature you can use unknown, physical, or digital.

total_billing_cycles.name: total_billing_cycles
total_billing_cycles.comment: Automatically terminate subscriptions after a defined number of billing cycles.  Number of billing cycles before the plan automatically stops renewing, defaults to null for continuous, automatic renewal.

trial_length.name: trial_length
trial_length.comment: Length of plan's trial period in trial_units. 0 means no trial. Default: 0

trial_unit.name: trial_unit
trial_unit.comment: Units for the plan's trial period. Default: "months" Enum: "days", "months"

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__subscription_add_on_history
metadata.comment: History of subscription add-ons to a particular plan.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: The date/time the add-on was last modified.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: The date/time the add-on was last created.

expired_at.name: expired_at
expired_at.comment: Date and time the subscription was/ will churn.

plan_add_on_id.name: plan_add_on_id
plan_add_on_id.comment: Plan add-on ID associated with subscription add-on ID.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: Number of add-ons added to the subscription.

unit_amount.name: unit_amount
unit_amount.comment: Amount of one unit of the add-on.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__subscription_change_history
metadata.comment: History of subscription changes, particularly upgrades and downgrades, but also changes to how subscription is invoiced.

activated.name: activated
activated.comment: Is the subscription change currently active?

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: When the subscription was last created.

deleted_at.name: deleted_at
deleted_at.comment: When subscription was rendered to be inactive.

plan_id.name: plan_id
plan_id.comment: Plan id associated with subscription change.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: Subscription ID associated with subscription change.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__subscription_history
metadata.comment: Subscriptions are created when your customers subscribe to one of your plans. The customer's subscription tells Recurly when and how much to bill the customer. The table holds the lineage of the subscription.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account this subscription belongs to.

add_ons_total.name: add_ons_total
add_ons_total.comment: Total price of add-ons. Greater or equal to 0.

canceled_at.name: canceled_at
canceled_at.comment: When the subscription was canceled. Can take future dates.

current_term_ended_at.name: current_term_ended_at
current_term_ended_at.comment: When the term ends. This is calculated by a plan's interval and total_billing_cycles in a term.  Subscription changes with a timeframe=renewal will be applied on this date.

current_term_started_at.name: current_term_started_at
current_term_started_at.comment: The start date of the term when the first billing period starts.  The subscription term is the length of time that a customer will be committed to a subscription.  A term can span multiple billing periods.

expires_at.name: expires_at
expires_at.comment: When the subscription has expired. Can take future dates.

has_auto_renew.name: has_auto_renew
has_auto_renew.comment: Whether the subscription renews at the end of its term. Default: true

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this subscription?

object.name: object
object.comment: The object type, in this case only "subscription"

paused_at.name: paused_at
paused_at.comment: Null unless subscription is paused or will pause at the end of the current billing period.

plan_id.name: plan_id
plan_id.comment: The plan this subscription belongs to.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: Subscription quantity. Greater or equal to 0.

remaining_billing_cycles.name: remaining_billing_cycles
remaining_billing_cycles.comment: The remaining billing cycles in the current term.

remaining_pause_cycles.name: remaining_pause_cycles
remaining_pause_cycles.comment: Null unless subscription is paused or will pause at the end of the current billing period.

renewal_billing_cycles.name: renewal_billing_cycles
renewal_billing_cycles.comment: If auto_renew=true, when a term completes, total_billing_cycles takes this value as the length of subsequent terms.  Defaults to the plan's total_billing_cycles.

state.name: state
state.comment: The current state of the subscription. Enum: "active", "canceled", "expired", "failed", "future", "paused"

total_billing_cycles.name: total_billing_cycles
total_billing_cycles.comment: The number of cycles/billing periods in a term.  When remaining_billing_cycles=0, if auto_renew=true the subscription will renew and a new term will begin,  otherwise the subscription will expire.

uuid.name: uuid
uuid.comment: The UUID is useful for matching data with the CSV exports and building URLs into Recurly's UI.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__transaction
metadata.comment: Purchasing information is sent to your payment gateway in an action called a transaction. This includes the customer's billing information and the amount of money to be charged, voided, or refunded.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: The account_id this transaction belongs to.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Total transaction amount sent to the payment gateway.

billing_postal_code.name: billing_postal_code
billing_postal_code.comment: Billing info - Zip or postal code.

collection_method.name: collection_method
collection_method.comment: The method by which the payment was collected. Enum: "automatic", "manual"

customer_message.name: customer_message
customer_message.comment: For declined (success=false) transactions, the message displayed to the customer.

gateway_approval_code.name: gateway_approval_code
gateway_approval_code.comment: Transaction approval code from the payment gateway.

gateway_message.name: gateway_message
gateway_message.comment: Transaction message from the payment gateway.

gateway_reference.name: gateway_reference
gateway_reference.comment: Transaction reference number from the payment gateway.

gateway_response_code.name: gateway_response_code
gateway_response_code.comment: For declined transactions (`success=false`), this field lists the gateway error code.

gateway_response_time.name: gateway_response_time
gateway_response_time.comment: Time, in seconds, for gateway to process the transaction.

gateway_response_values.name: gateway_response_values
gateway_response_values.comment: The values in this field will vary from gateway to gateway.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The invoice_id this transaction belongs to.

is_refunded.name: is_refunded
is_refunded.comment: Indicates if part or all of this transaction was refunded.

is_successful.name: is_successful
is_successful.comment: Did this transaction complete successfully? True or False

is_most_recent_record.name: is_most_recent_record
is_most_recent_record.comment: Is this the most recent record of this transaction?

origin.name: origin
origin.comment: Describes how the transaction was triggered. Enum: "api", "chargeback", "force_collect", "hpp", "merchant", "recurly_admin", "recurlyjs", "recurring", "refunded_externally", "transparent"

original_transaction_id.name: original_transaction_id
original_transaction_id.comment: If this transaction is a refund (type=refund), this will be the ID of the original transaction on the invoice being refunded.

status.name: status
status.comment: The current transaction status. Note that the status may change, e.g. a pending transaction may become declined or success may later become void. Enum: "chargeback", "declined", "error", "pending", "processing", "scheduled", "success", "void"

status_message.name: status_message
status_message.comment: For declined (success=false) transactions, the message displayed to the merchant.

type.name: type
type.comment:
Transaction types:
  authorization – verifies billing information and places a hold on money in the customer's account.
  capture – captures funds held by an authorization and completes a purchase.
  purchase – combines the authorization and capture in one transaction.
  refund – returns all or a portion of the money collected in a previous transaction to the customer.
  verify – a $0 or $1 transaction used to verify billing information which is immediately voided.
Enum: "authorization", "capture", "purchase", "refund", "verify"

uuid.name: uuid
uuid.comment: The UUID is useful for matching data with the CSV exports and building URLs into Recurly's UI.

voided_by_invoice_id.name: voided_by_invoice_id
voided_by_invoice_id.comment: The invoice_id this transaction was voided.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_recurly__transaction_subscription
metadata.comment: The connection between transaction and subscription. FK on transaction is transaction_id, and on subscription is subscription_id.

transaction_id.name: transaction_id
transaction_id.comment: The id of the transaction associated with the subscription.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: The id of the subscription associated with the transaction.
